# artoo

> A lightweight, lossless, CDISC-native clinical-dataset reader/writer for R.
> One canonical metadata model (`artoo_meta`) is carried by every codec, so
> any-to-any conversion across SAS XPORT, CDISC Dataset-JSON v1.1, NDJSON,
> Apache Parquet, and RDS is lossless by construction. The package a clinical
> R programmer reaches for instead of a generic labelled-frame reader.

The workflow is one line of three verbs: read a spec, apply it to a raw data
frame, then read or write any format. Every condition is a typed
`artoo_error_<kind>` / `artoo_warning_<kind>` / `artoo_message_<kind>`.

## Specification
- `artoo_spec(datasets, variables, codelists, ...)`: build and validate a CDISC spec from metadata tables.
- `read_spec(path, datasets =, on_duplicate =)`: read a spec from native JSON, a Pinnacle 21 xlsx workbook, or Define-XML; scope to chosen datasets; choose a duplicate-row policy.
- `write_spec(spec, path)`: write a spec to JSON (lossless) or xlsx (interchange).
- `set_type(spec, dataset, VAR = type, ...)`: retype a variable in R when the data disagrees with the spec's dataType, instead of editing the workbook.
- `repair_spec(spec, findings)`: flip every `integer_fraction` / `integer_overflow` variable to `float` from a findings frame, in one call.
- `spec_datasets()`, `spec_variables()`, `spec_codelists()`, `spec_keys()`, `spec_standard()`, `spec_study()`, `spec_methods()`, `spec_comments()`, `spec_documents()`: read one slot of a spec.

## Conform
- `apply_spec(x, spec, dataset, conformance =, na_position =, extra =, on_coercion_loss =)`: coerce, order, sort, and stamp a raw frame to its spec. `extra = "drop"` trims to the spec's columns; `on_coercion_loss = "keep"` preserves data an `integer` dataType cannot hold (the divergence is reported, not silently truncated).
- `decode_column(x, spec, dataset, variable, direction =, no_match =)`: map a coded variable through a spec codelist.

## Check
- `check_spec(x, spec, dataset)`: conformance findings for one dataset (needs the data).
- `check_study(spec, data = named_list)`: conformance findings across a whole study in one pass; prints a dataset-by-check count matrix; feeds `repair_spec()`.
- `validate_spec(spec)`: structural integrity of the spec itself (no data).
- `conformance(x)`: read the findings `apply_spec()` attached to a frame.
- `artoo_checks(...)`: toggle which conformance dimensions run. Only `integer_fraction` / `integer_overflow` are fatal coercion checks; `type_mismatch` is informational.

## Read and write (lossless any-to-any)
- `read_dataset()` / `write_dataset()`: generic, dispatch on file extension.
- `read_xpt()` / `write_xpt()`: SAS XPORT v5/v8.
- `read_json()` / `write_json()`, `read_ndjson()` / `write_ndjson()`: CDISC Dataset-JSON v1.1, array and newline-delimited.
- `read_parquet()` / `write_parquet()`: Apache Parquet.
- `read_rds()` / `write_rds()`: R serialization.
- Every reader takes `encoding =` (`read_xpt()` auto-detects windows-1252 when bytes are not valid UTF-8); every writer takes `on_invalid = c("error", "replace", "ignore")`.

## Inspect and metadata
- `columns()`: the SAS PROC CONTENTS variable pane.
- `members()` / `xpt_members()`: the dataset inventory of a file or library.
- `get_meta()` / `set_meta()` / `sync_meta()`: the `artoo_meta` a conformed frame carries.
- `artoo_formats()` / `artoo_encodings()`: reference tables of codecs and charset names.

## Demo data
Bundled CDISC pilot specs (`adam_spec`, `sdtm_spec`) and datasets (`cdisc_adsl`, `cdisc_adae`, `cdisc_dm`, `cdisc_vs`, `cdisc_ts`, `cdisc_suppdm`), all rebuilt from public sources.
